goal:
Rework evencursor for Even glasses + Cursor agents

Simplify the overloaded first version of evencursor (voice-first Even Realities HUD for Cursor agents).

- Cursor: use https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/api/endpoints for list/detail/status and all agent interaction.
- Auth: Sign in with Cursor only; no extra API tokens.
- Even: https://hub.evenrealities.com/docs/getting-started/overview as primary docs.
- Voice: Deepgram streaming STT; recognize slash commands (e.g. /orchestrate).
- UI: glasses-friendly layout with buttons and voice where needed.
- Process: small granular commits; subagents where possible.
- Models: root/subplanners claude-opus-4-7; all workers composer-2.5-fast in plan.json.
summary: rework evencursor for Even glasses + Cursor cloud agents

tasks.name: foundation-protocol-and-docs

tasks.name: cursor-cloud-client
tasks.scopedGoal: Replace the `cursor-agent` CLI driver with a typed REST client for the Cursor Cloud Agents API and wire it into the bridge.
tasks.brief:
## Reference

- https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/api/endpoints — v1 Agents API (Basic Auth with API key as username, empty password).
- https://cursor.com/docs/api — auth + rate limits.

All Cursor calls use `https://api.cursor.com` with header `Authorization: Basic ${base64(apiKey + ':')}`.

## What you own

Delete `src/bridge/cursor-runner.ts`. Add a typed client module and a session manager that uses it.

- `src/bridge/cursor-client.ts` — narrow REST helper:
  - `me(apiKey)` → `GET /v1/me`. Returns `CursorIdentity` or throws on 401.
  - `createAgent({ apiKey, prompt, repoUrl, startingRef, model, autoCreatePR })` → `POST /v1/agents`.
  - `listAgents({ apiKey, limit, cursor })` → `GET /v1/agents`.
  - `getAgent({ apiKey, id })` → `GET /v1/agents/{id}`.
  - `createRun({ apiKey, agentId, prompt, mode })` → `POST /v1/agents/{id}/runs`.
  - `getRun({ apiKey, agentId, runId })` → `GET /v1/agents/{id}/runs/{runId}`.
  - `cancelRun({ apiKey, agentId, runId })` → `POST /v1/agents/{id}/runs/{runId}/cancel`.
  - `streamRun({ apiKey, agentId, runId, signal, onEvent })` → SSE client that parses `status`, `assistant`, `thinking`, `tool_call`, `heartbeat`, `result`, `done`, `error`. Use `globalThis.fetch` + `ReadableStream` SSE parsing; respect `Last-Event-ID` on reconnect; treat `410 stream_expired` by falling back to `getRun`.
- `src/bridge/session-manager.ts` — owns the local `AgentSession` map. Exposes:
  - `createSession(prompt, opts)` — calls `createAgent`, persists session, kicks off background stream.
  - `answerSession(sessionId, text)` — calls `createRun` with follow-up prompt.
  - `cancelSession(sessionId)` — calls `cancelRun` if a run is active.
  - `snapshot()` / `subscribe(listener)` for the WS layer.
  - Heuristic question extraction from streamed `assistant` text (port the v1 logic; one concise line per question; deduplicate).
  - Maps `CloudAgentRunStatus` → `AgentInboxStatus`.

## Bridge integration

Update `src/bridge/server.ts`:
- Drop all references to `cursor-runner`, child process, worktree, `CURSOR_AGENT_BIN`, `EVENCURSOR_DEFAULT_WORKSPACE`.
- Hold the API key in a `BridgeAuth` singleton (set by the sign-in slice; default `undefined`).
- Route handlers:
  - `POST /api/sessions` — requires signed-in auth; calls `SessionManager.createSession`.
  - `POST /api/sessions/:id/answer` — requires auth; calls `SessionManager.answerSession`.
  - `POST /api/sessions/:id/cancel` — requires auth; cancels.
  - `GET /api/sessions` — list snapshot.
  - `GET /api/repositories` — proxy `/v1/repositories` (best-effort, log rate-limit failures and return cached empty list).
  - `GET /api/models` — proxy `/v1/models`.
  - `GET /api/auth` — returns `BridgeAuthState`.
  - Unauthed requests get `401 { error: "signin_required" }`.
- Keep the `/ws` snapshot/session.updated stream.
- Add a tiny in-memory secret holder set by `/api/auth/cursor` (the sign-in slice will write that endpoint). Export a `setApiKey(key)` symbol the auth slice will call.

## Acceptance

- `src/bridge/cursor-runner.ts` is deleted.
- All requests carry the Authorization header derived from the in-memory key.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- A new vitest file covers the status → inbox mapping and the SSE parser with mocked events.

## Commit hygiene

One commit per file, plus one for tests. Push the branch.
acceptance: src/bridge/cursor-runner.ts removed; new cursor-client.ts + session-manager.ts present
All Cursor API calls go to https://api.cursor.com/v1/* with Basic auth
Bridge enforces signed-in state before mutating session endpoints
SSE stream parser handles status/assistant/thinking/tool_call/result/done/error and falls back on 410 stream_expired
npm run typecheck passes
npm test passes

## Manual

tasks.name: auth-signin-cursor
tasks.scopedGoal: Implement Sign in with Cursor end-to-end: bridge endpoint that validates the user-supplied API key against /v1/me, plus a hub sign-in screen. No other tokens are surfaced to the user.
tasks.brief:
## Reference
- Cursor API auth: https://cursor.com/docs/api (Basic auth, user API key from https://cursor.com/dashboard/integrations).
- `/v1/me` returns `{ apiKeyName, createdAt, userEmail }`.

## Bridge

In `src/bridge/server.ts` (and a new `src/bridge/auth.ts` if it helps):
- `POST /api/auth/cursor` — body `{ apiKey: string }`. Call `cursorClient.me(apiKey)`. On success, store the key in process memory only (never log, never persist), set `BridgeAuthState.signedIn = true`, set `identity`, return `200 { identity }`. On 401, return `401 { error: "invalid_key" }`.
- `DELETE /api/auth/cursor` — clears in-memory key, emits a broadcast event `auth.changed`.
- `GET /api/auth` — returns `{ signedIn, identity }`.
- Broadcast `auth.changed` over `/ws` whenever the state flips.
- Treat the key as a session-only secret: do not write it to disk, do not echo it in error messages, do not include it in any handoff.

## Hub

Add a sign-in gate to `src/hub/main.ts` (or split into `src/hub/auth.ts`):
- Before showing the inbox, fetch `/api/auth`. If `signedIn=false`, render the sign-in panel: large input field for the API key, a `Sign in with Cursor` button, a short explainer with a link to https://cursor.com/dashboard/integrations.
- On submit, POST to `/api/auth/cursor`. On success, swap to the inbox view and show the user email pill.
- Add a sign-out button to the inbox header.
- Persist a `signedIn` hint only (no key) in `sessionStorage` so the gate reads correctly during reconnects.
- The sign-in panel must be readable on a phone held at arm's length: 18px+ inputs, 48px+ buttons.

## Acceptance

- The user never enters or sees Deepgram, Even, or any other token.
- Refreshing the page while signed in shows the inbox immediately; after server restart, the user is asked to sign in again (memory-only key is intentional).
- `npm run typecheck` and `npm test` pass.
- A new test covers the auth route happy + invalid-key paths with a mocked fetch.

Commit hygiene: one commit per file plus one for tests; push the branch.
acceptance: POST /api/auth/cursor validates against /v1/me and stores key in memory only
Hub renders sign-in screen when not authenticated and inbox after sign-in
Sign-out clears the in-memory key and forces a fresh sign-in
No other API tokens are exposed in the UI
npm run typecheck passes
npm test passes

## Automated

## Manual
- Start bridge + hub, attempt to load inbox without signing in (expect sign-in screen), then POST a bogus key (expect 401), then a valid key (expect inbox).

tasks.name: voice-substrate
tasks.scopedGoal: Build the Deepgram streaming voice substrate end-to-end: bridge proxy that holds the Deepgram key, hub mic capture, slash-command recognition (/orchestrate and friends), and Even glasses audio routing.
tasks.brief:
## Slice scope

This slice owns voice from mic to command. Decompose into a small set of worker tasks (recommend ~3) and aggregate handoffs back to the root.

## References
- Deepgram streaming docs: https://developers.deepgram.com/docs/getting-started-with-live-streaming-audio (and `wss://api.deepgram.com/v1/listen?model=nova-2-general&encoding=linear16&sample_rate=16000`).
- Even SDK audio: `bridge.audioControl(true)` opens the mic; PCM bytes arrive as `event.audioEvent.audioPcm` (Uint8Array). See https://hub.evenrealities.com/docs/getting-started/overview and the npm README (`@evenrealities/even_hub_sdk` 0.0.10).

## What must exist when you hand back

- `src/bridge/voice.ts` — WS endpoint `/ws/voice` that:
  - Auths against the signed-in bridge state.
  - Accepts a binary stream of 16kHz mono Int16 PCM frames.
  - Maintains an outbound WS to Deepgram using server-side `DEEPGRAM_API_KEY`; relays Deepgram JSON transcripts back to the client as parsed `{ type: "transcript", isFinal, text }` and `{ type: "command", name, args }` messages.
  - Slash command grammar: recognize `/orchestrate <args>`, `/answer <args>`, `/cancel`, `/sign-out`; everything else is a free transcript.
- `src/shared/commands.ts` — pure function `parseCommand(text)` returning `VoiceCommand`.
- `src/hub/voice.ts` — replaces `src/hub/speech.ts`:
  - Browser path: `getUserMedia` → `AudioWorklet` resampling to 16kHz Int16 → WS to `/ws/voice`.
  - Glasses path: when running inside Even Hub WebView (detected via `waitForEvenAppBridge`), call `bridge.audioControl(true)` and forward `audioEvent.audioPcm` frames to the same WS.
  - Surface a single `onTranscript(text, isFinal)` + `onCommand(cmd)` API consumed by the inbox view.
- `src/hub/speech.ts` — deleted.

## Constraints

- The Deepgram key never reaches the browser. Read it from `process.env.DEEPGRAM_API_KEY` on the bridge; fail the `/ws/voice` upgrade with a clear close code if it's missing.
- Slash command parsing must be unit-tested; live in `src/shared/commands.ts` so both bridge and hub can use it.
- Bridge typecheck + tests must pass at the end of every worker handoff in your subtree.

## Suggested decomposition (you decide)

1. `voice-bridge-proxy` — bridge `/ws/voice` + Deepgram relay + tests.
2. `voice-commands` — shared `parseCommand` + tests.
3. `voice-hub-capture` — browser + Even glasses mic capture, replaces speech.ts.

## Final handoff

Report the deliverable branch (likely the integration branch from the last task in your subtree), measurements (`LOC src/hub/voice.ts`, `LOC src/bridge/voice.ts`), and any follow-ups for the integration slice.
acceptance: Bridge owns the Deepgram key; the hub never sees it
Slash commands /orchestrate, /answer, /cancel, /sign-out are recognized and unit-tested
Hub captures browser mic via AudioWorklet at 16kHz Int16 PCM
Glasses path uses bridge.audioControl + audioEvent.audioPcm forwarding
src/hub/speech.ts is removed
npm run typecheck and npm test pass on the deliverable branch
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `npm install` once.

## Automated
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npm test`

## Manual
- Without DEEPGRAM_API_KEY, /ws/voice upgrades fail loudly.
- With the key, a dummy PCM stream produces transcript and command messages downstream.

tasks.name: ui-glasses-layout
tasks.scopedGoal: Redesign the hub UI for glasses-friendly use and rebuild the Even HUD output so both surfaces stay glanceable. Use buttons where touch makes sense and voice where it's faster.
tasks.brief:
## What you own

- `src/hub/main.ts` — top-level layout.
- `src/hub/styles.css` — typography, contrast, spacing for arms-length reading.
- `src/hub/even.ts` — HUD rendering via the Even SDK (`waitForEvenAppBridge`, `createStartUpPageContainer` first time, `rebuildPageContainer` thereafter, `textContainerUpgrade` for live text). Constraints: 576×288 px canvas per eye, 4-bit greyscale, text-only is fine.

## Layout requirements

Phone / hub view:
- Single column, max 3 panels visible without scrolling: HUD summary, Needs You, Sessions.
- Buttons: minimum 48×48 tap target, primary buttons full-width.
- A persistent voice button anchored bottom-right (icon + label) that:
  - Press = push-to-talk.
  - Long-press = continuous listening until release.
  - Calls the voice substrate's `onCommand` / `onTranscript` callbacks.
- A slash-command bar above the keyboard with quick-tap chips: `/orchestrate`, `/answer`, `/cancel`.
- Sessions list shows `inboxStatus` as a colored pill (`running` blue, `waiting` amber, `done` green, `failed` red) plus model badge.

Even HUD:
- Use `summarizeForHud` (already returns 3 short strings) and render them in a 3-line text stack.
- First HUD bring-up: `createStartUpPageContainer({ containerTotalNum: 3, textObject: [titleLine, bodyLine, metaLine] })`. Subsequent updates: `textContainerUpgrade` per container, falling back to `rebuildPageContainer` when the line set changes shape.
- Cap each line at 48 chars (the SDK accepts longer, but the display ergonomics demand this).
- Detect `launchSource === "glassesMenu"` to skip the phone-only sign-in modal and instead show a minimal `Sign in on phone` HUD line until the bridge reports signed-in.

## Acceptance

- No reliance on Web Speech API in the hub bundle.
- Color/contrast meets WCAG AA at 18px body text.
- Even HUD calls `createStartUpPageContainer` exactly once per session and `rebuildPageContainer`/`textContainerUpgrade` thereafter.
- `npm run typecheck` and `npm test` pass.
- Snapshot test for `summarizeForHud` still passes (do not change the function's contract).

Commit hygiene: split layout, styles, Even HUD, and voice wiring into separate commits.
acceptance: Hub renders three panels with 48px+ tap targets and a voice button
Slash-command chips post /orchestrate, /answer, /cancel via the voice substrate
Even HUD bring-up uses createStartUpPageContainer once, then textContainerUpgrade
src/hub/speech.ts is not referenced anywhere
npm run typecheck passes
npm test passes

tasks.name: integration-merge
tasks.scopedGoal: Merge cursor-cloud-client, auth-signin-cursor, voice-substrate, and ui-glasses-layout into a single integration branch. Resolve conflicts, run the full typecheck + test suite, and open a single integration PR.
tasks.brief:
## Inputs

Upstream branches (the script will paste each upstream handoff into your prompt; trust the `## Branch` line in each):
- cursor-cloud-client
- auth-signin-cursor
- voice-substrate
- ui-glasses-layout

## Merge strategy

1. Start from `orch/evencursor/cursor-cloud-client` (the bridge spine).
2. Merge `auth-signin-cursor`. Conflicts likely in `src/bridge/server.ts`; prefer the auth slice's route surface and re-apply cursor-cloud-client's route additions on top.
3. Merge `voice-substrate` (subplanner deliverable branch). Conflicts likely in `src/bridge/server.ts` (route table) and `src/shared/protocol.ts` (`BridgeEvent` union). Union both sides; keep `voice.transcript` / `voice.command` events.
4. Merge `ui-glasses-layout`. Conflicts likely in `src/hub/main.ts`, `src/hub/styles.css`, `src/hub/even.ts`. Prefer ui-glasses-layout's structure; re-wire the voice substrate hooks (`onTranscript`/`onCommand`) and the sign-in gate after each merge.
5. Run `npm install && npm run typecheck && npm test` between merges; do not advance with a broken tree.

## After merging

- Hand-sanity-check the README / docs to make sure they reflect the merged behavior (sign-in gate, voice flow, glasses HUD).
- Open a single draft PR against `main` titled "evencursor: rework for Cursor cloud agents + Even glasses + Deepgram voice". Body must list each upstream slice with its branch name and PR (if any), plus a short integration testing summary.

## Acceptance

- A single merge commit (or fast-forward chain) ties all four slices together.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm test` passes.
- One integration PR exists, draft, against main.

## Measurements

Report:
- `LOC(src)` via `git ls-files src | xargs wc -l | tail -n1`.
- `tests-passed` via `npm test -- --reporter=verbose` (parse `Tests` line).
acceptance: All four upstream branches are merged into the integration branch
npm run typecheck passes on the integration branch
npm test passes on the integration branch
A single draft PR targets main with all slices listed in the body

measurements.name: LOC(src)
measurements.command: git ls-files src | xargs wc -l | tail -n1 | awk '{print $1}'
